def solve(grid : Array(Array(Int64))) : Int64
  M = grid.len
  N = grid[0].len
  K_MAX = 1024

  # DP[i][j][x] is true if XOR sum x is achievable at (i, j).
  # We store the reachable XOR sums (0 to 1023) in a boolean array of size 1024.
  DP = Array(M) { |i|
    Array(N) { |j|
      Array(K_MAX) { |k| false }
    }
  }

  # Base case (0, 0)
  initialXOR = grid[0][0]
  # We store the XOR sum as an Int index (since it must be < 1024)
  DP[0][0][initialXOR.to_i] = true

  # Fill the first row
  for j in 1...N
    G = grid[0][j]
    for x in 0..<K_MAX
      if DP[0][j - 1][x]
        newXOR = x.to_i ^ G.to_i
        DP[0][j][newXOR] = true
  end

  # Fill the first column
  for i in 1...M
    G = grid[i][0]
    for x in 0..<K_MAX
      if DP[i - 1][0][x]
        newXOR = x.to_i ^ G.to_i
        DP[i][0][newXOR] = true
  end

  # Fill the rest of the table
  for i in 1...M
    for j in 1...N
      G = grid[i][j]
      
      # 1. From top (i-1, j)
      for x in 0..<K_MAX
        if DP[i - 1][j][x]
          newXOR = x.to_i ^ G.to_i
          DP[i][j][newXOR] = true
        end
      end

      # 2. From left (i, j-1)
      for x in 0..<K_MAX
        if DP[i][j - 1][x]
          newXOR = x.to_i ^ G.to_i
          DP[i][j][newXOR] = true
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # Find the minimum achievable XOR value at (M-1, N-1)
  for x in 0..<K_MAX
    if DP[M - 1][N - 1][x]
      return x.to_i64
    end
  end
  
  # Should be unreachable given problem constraints
  return 0.to_i64
end
```
